steps.prompt: How many finite verbs are in this sentence?

hints.one (est): There are two verbs: 'est' (is) and 'erit' (will be)
hints.three: Only 'est' and 'erit' are finite verbs — two total
hints.none: Both 'est' and 'erit' are finite verbs of 'esse'

hints.to eat: That would be 'edere' — 'est' here is from 'esse' (to be)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'est' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'fuit' — 'est' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'erit' — that is the other verb in this sentence

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'est' — 'erit' with -eri- is future of 'esse'
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'erit' is future indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'fuit' — 'erit' is future indicative

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat)
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'thesaurum' — 'thesaurus' is nominative subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'thesauri' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'thesauri' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'thesauri' ends in -i

hints.accusative: While 'cor' looks identical in accusative, it is the subject of 'erit' — nominative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'cordis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'cordis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'cordis' ends in -is

steps.prompt: What is the function of 'Ubi' and 'ibi'?

hints.relative and demonstrative pronouns: 'Ubi' and 'ibi' are adverbs of place, not pronouns
hints.interrogative and indefinite: 'Ubi' can be interrogative, but with 'ibi' they form a correlative pair

translation: Where your treasure is, there your heart will be
